title: Local-first principle
text: PDF, notes, chunks, embeddings, chat history, wiki pages, and graph data are stored on the user's machine. Models can be routed through Ollama, making the system useful for privacy-sensitive study and low-connectivity environments. Cloud (Gemini) is opt-in via explicit API key configuration; the local path is the default.

title: PDF → cited answers + wiki + graph
text: Pipeline: PDF upload → embedded text extraction → OCR fallback for weak pages → page-aware chunking → local embeddings → hybrid retrieval → Gemma/Ollama reasoning → cited answers → Book Wiki generation → concept graph extraction → study guide generation.

title: Structured study artifacts
text: In the Book Wiki workflow the local model receives chunked PDF sections and produces structured study artifacts including section summaries, key terms, claims, methods, equations, people, datasets, and concept relationships. Outputs stored in SQLite as wiki pages, graph nodes, and graph edges.

title: Tauri 2 + React 19 + Rust + SQLite
text: CiteMind is built as a Tauri 2 desktop app with a React 19/TypeScript frontend and a Rust backend. SQLite is used as the local data layer for papers, PDF chunks, embeddings, wiki pages, graph nodes, graph edges, bookmarks, notes, chat history, and settings.

title: Every answer carries page anchors
text: The model surfaces page numbers + section anchors in every answer. If a question cannot be answered from the document, the assistant declines rather than fabricates. Citation-back-to-page is the central UX move that distinguishes a research wiki from a generic chatbot.

title: Persistent learning layer
text: Learning is not only about receiving one answer; it is about seeing how ideas connect, checking evidence, returning to sources, and building memory over time. CiteMind keeps each PDF as a persistent learning layer the learner can revisit, search, and build on.

title: Live concept graph
text: Concepts extracted from chunked PDF sections are stored as graph nodes with relationships (graph edges). The graph supports exploration: nodes link to source pages, edges represent claims/dependencies. Persistent in SQLite; visualizable in the frontend.

title: Education access + privacy posture
text: Designed for students, researchers, and self-learners who need help understanding difficult documents without depending on cloud AI tools or surrendering privacy. Particularly useful for learners with limited cloud access, private documents, or low-connectivity environments.

title: Education + privacy + local AI
text: CiteMind fits the Gemma 4 Good theme by focusing on education access, privacy, and local AI. Demonstrates how Gemma-family models running through Ollama can help people understand complex material on their own machine. Generic chatbot → persistent learning layer around the source document.

title: Three reusable patterns
text: Three reusable design patterns generalize from CiteMind to the rest of the catalog: (1) local-first with cited answers — privacy-preserving + ground-truth-respecting; (2) source document → persistent knowledge layer — the document is the persistent state, not the chat; (3) concept graph from text — relationships extracted as first-class entities, queryable + visualizable.
